title: JournalEvent
description: One line of a run journal's events.jsonl — the append-only provenance record (ARCHITECTURE.md §4). Each property carries an x-conformance marker: 'normative' fields form the cross-runner conformance set (§3.3), 'excluded' fields (timestamps, durations, the runner.* namespace) are never compared. This classification drives #29's determinism assertion and the V2 conformance harness (#40).

schema.description: Versioned envelope id. A reader branches on this; a newer version is read leniently (unknown fields/types tolerated).

seq.description: Monotonic per-run sequence number from 1. The primary ordering key; at tier 3 events order by (branch, seq).

type.description: Event kind. trigger.fired, tick.skipped, workflow.starved, claim lifecycle/lock, and config.* events are instance-journal-only (scheduler/events.jsonl, ARCHITECTURE.md §4/§6); the rest appear in a run's own events.jsonl (run.started/run.finished are reused in both streams).
type.x-conformance: conditional: stage.heartbeat and gate.started are excluded; all other event types are normative unless separately excluded

branch.description: Parallel-branch id. 0 at tiers 1-2; reserved for tier-3 parallel branches. Secondary ordering key.

time.description: When the event was recorded. Excluded from conformance.

stage.description: Stage name for stage.* events and stage-scoped artifacts.
stage.x-conformance: conditional: excluded for stage.heartbeat; otherwise normative

attempt.description: 1-based attempt number for stage.* events and stage-scoped artifacts.
attempt.x-conformance: conditional: excluded for stage.heartbeat; otherwise normative

attemptClass.description: Why a retry attempt exists. Only 'policy' attempts are normative; 'infra' attempts are excluded from the conformance set. The initial attempt carries no class and is always included.
attemptClass.x-conformance: conditional: excluded for stage.heartbeat and when 'infra'

gate.description: Gate name for gate.* events. gate.started is recovery bookkeeping and excluded from conformance; gate.evaluated is normative.

outputs.description: Small, named scalar values a stage.finished's ResultEnvelope.Outputs carried (docs/stage-contract.md) — journaled so a resumed run can reconstruct a finished stage's result (its lastStage/lastResult, or a resumed gate's subject) without it having lived only in the runner's process memory.

artifacts.description: A stage.finished's ResultEnvelope.Artifacts — the pointers this attempt produced — for the same reconstruction reason as outputs.
artifacts.x-conformance: conditional: each entry's digest is normative, path/size/mediaType are not

name.description: Label for a Ref (artifact/input name) or the redaction target.

redaction.description: Old->new digest remediation for redaction events.

runner.description: Runner-specific annotations. The ONLY sanctioned runner-specific divergence and ALWAYS excluded from conformance.

workflow.description: Instance-journal only: workflow name for a scheduler decision (trigger.fired, tick.skipped, or an instance-level run.started/run.finished echo). Not present in a run's own events.jsonl.
workflow.x-conformance: not applicable (instance journal, not a run's conformance set)

gaggle.description: Instance-journal only: gaggle that scopes the workflow name.
gaggle.x-conformance: not applicable (instance journal, not a run's conformance set)

runId.description: Instance-journal only: the run a scheduler decision or claim transition pertains to.
runId.x-conformance: not applicable (instance journal, not a run's conformance set)

reason.description: Instance-journal only: short, stable explanation for a tick.skipped or workflow.starved decision.
reason.x-conformance: not applicable (instance journal, not a run's conformance set)

skipCount.description: Instance-journal only: consecutive shared-pool refusal count at a workflow.starved threshold crossing.
skipCount.x-conformance: not applicable (instance journal, not a run's conformance set)

path.description: Blob location relative to the run directory. Runner-arranged; not normative.

digest.description: Content address. Normative except for runner-assembled context-manifest artifacts, whose bytes include non-normative pointer metadata.
digest.x-conformance: conditional: excluded for runner-assembled context-manifest artifacts

size.description: Blob length in bytes. Derived; not normative.

mediaType.description: Optional media type (parity with the wire ArtifactPointer). Not normative.

url.description: Convenience link. Not normative — identity is (provider, kind, id).

message.description: Human-facing detail. Excluded (varies run to run).
